steps.title: Build two rows for the house value
steps.content: At $x=0$, the house is worth $196{,}000$. A $3.5\%$ yearly increase means multiply by $1.035$ after one year, so the next value is $196{,}000(1.035)=202{,}860$.

steps.title: Find the exponential pattern
steps.content: An exponential growth model has the form $f(x)=a(b)^x$, where $a$ is the starting value and $b$ is the yearly multiplier. Here, $a=196{,}000$ and $b=1.035$, so $f(x)=196{,}000(1.035)^x$.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content: Choice A — $f(x) = 196,000(0.035)^x$ uses $0.035$ as the whole multiplier, and Choice B — $f(x) = 196,000(0.35)^x$ uses $0.35$; both are less than $1$, so they model decreases. Choice D — $f(x) = 196,000(1.35)^x$ uses $1.35$, which is a $35\%$ increase. Choice C — $f(x) = 196,000(1.035)^x$ uses $1.035$, the growth multiplier for a $3.5\%$ increase. Choice C — $f(x) = 196,000(1.035)^x$.
